seed_flags.rationale: Touches the UoA-sensitive topic of Liability Limitations and Exclusions; exact alignment is not obvious from the clause text. Contract Manager should confirm against: Preferred: Total liability (including under indemnities offered) limited to the lesser of: (a) amount paid under the contract / contract value (subject to liability limit in Research Contract Delegations) OR (b) NZD$500,000. Limited to direct losses caused by negligent acts/omissions or wilful misconduct of the University. Consequential or indirect losses, 'hold harmless', and loss of data/profit/revenue/savings/business/goodwill explicitly excluded. | Acceptable: Total liability limit (including indemnities) in line with Research Contract Delegations. Uncapped liability acceptable ONLY for Trusted Funders on contestable rounds. Indirect losses acceptable only if total exposure is capped per Delegations. Carve-outs from cap acceptable for: Fraud, Wilful default, Personal injury/death, statutory non-excludable liability, and confidentiality (only if client revenue < NZD$150M). | Escalation: Refer Research Grants and Contracts Delegations (and Portfolio Manager for Trusted Funders on non-contestable rounds).

augmented_flags.clause_title: a. Is within the public domain or subsequently enters the public domain w

No direct reference to confidentiality exclusions or similar provisions exists in the Positions document.

augmented_flags.clause_title: b. At the date of disclosure to the receiving Party is lawfully known to
augmented_flags.snippet: b. At the date of disclosure to the receiving Party is lawfully known to the receiving Party, unless both the receiving Party and the disclosing Party agree that it should remain confidential;
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause deviates materially from UoA Position #POS-11 on Confidentiality. The Preferred Position requires that confidential information excludes information already public, independently developed, previously known by the receiving party, or legally required to be disclosed. This clause introduces an additional condition requiring mutual agreement between the parties for such information to remain confidential, which is not aligned with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable tiers. This creates ambiguity and could impose unnecessary restrictions on UoA's ability to manage confidentiality. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts, is required.

augmented_flags.clause_title: c. Is mutually agreed by the disclosing Party and the receiving Party not
augmented_flags.snippet: c. Is mutually agreed by the disclosing Party and the receiving Party not to be confidential; or
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses the definition of confidential information, which is a UoA-sensitive topic under Position #POS-11. While the clause allows for mutual agreement to exclude certain information from confidentiality, it does not explicitly align with the Preferred Position, which requires exclusions for information already public, independently developed, previously known, or legally required to be disclosed. The clause also does not address UoA's ability to disclose to agents and Affiliates or the duration of confidentiality obligations. This deviation places the clause in the Acceptable tier, but it requires careful review to ensure it does not conflict with UoA's broader confidentiality requirements.

This topic is not explicitly covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or tied to any specific UoA template. The candidate flag incorrectly associates this clause with Dispute Resolution (POS-18), which is unrelated to the content of the clause.

augmented_flags.clause_title: Project IP, in respect of a Project, means Intellectual Property develope
augmented_flags.snippet: Project IP, in respect of a Project, means Intellectual Property developed by or on behalf of AUL and or either of them, during the course of that Project, but excludes (for the avoidance of doubt) Background IP.
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause pertains to Intellectual Property, which is explicitly outside the scope of UoA's Preferred Contracting Positions. All IP clauses must be referred to Auckland UniServices (Head of IP) for review, as per UoA Position #POS-IP. This clause does not trigger escalation but requires UniServices' input.

augmented_flags.snippet: 2. RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE PARTIES

augmented_flags.clause_title: 2.1 Nothing in this Agreement shall be read or construed as creating a pa
augmented_flags.snippet: 2.1 Nothing in this Agreement shall be read or construed as creating a partnership of any kind, joint venture or trust (each Party being individually responsible only for its obligations as set out in this Agreement and...
augmented_flags.rationale: The clause addresses the relationship between the parties, explicitly stating that no partnership, joint venture, or trust is created, and that each party is individually responsible for its obligations. It also limits the authority of each party to act as an agent or bind the other party, except as expressly agreed in writing. While it mentions warranties, it does so only to prohibit one party from giving guarantees, warranties, or representations on behalf of the other party without express written permission. This aligns with UoA Position #POS-10, which requires the exclusion of all other warranties beyond the University's authority to enter into the contract and performance to a professional standard. The clause does not introduce any additional warranties and is therefore consistent with the Preferred tier.
